class FenwickTree
    def initialize(size)
        @tree = Array.new(size + 1, 0)
    end

    def update(pos, delta)
        while pos <= @tree.size
            @tree[pos] += delta
            pos += pos & -pos
        end
    end

    def query(pos)
        res = 0
        while pos > 0
            res += @tree[pos]
            pos -= pos & -pos
        end
        res
    end
end

def compute_depth(x)
    depth = 0
    while x != 1
        x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
        depth += 1
    end
    depth
end

def solve(nums, queries)
    n = nums.length
    depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }
    ft = Array.new(7) { FenwickTree.new(n) }
    depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
        ft[d].update(i + 1, 1)
    end
    answer = []
    queries.each do |q|
        if q[0] == 1
            l = q[1]
            r = q[2]
            k = q[3]
            ft_k = ft[k]
            sum_r = ft_k.query(r + 1)
            sum_l = ft_k.query(l)
            answer << (sum_r - sum_l)
        else
            idx = q[1]
            val = q[2]
            old_d = depths[idx]
            new_d = compute_depth(val)
            ft[old_d].update(idx + 1, -1)
            ft[new_d].update(idx + 1, 1)
            depths[idx] = new_d
            nums[idx] = val
        end
    end
    answer
end

class FenwickTree
    def initialize(size)
        @tree = Array.new(size + 1, 0)
    end

    def update(pos, delta)
        while pos <= @tree.size
            @tree[pos] += delta
            pos += pos & -pos
        end
    end

    def query(pos)
        res = 0
        while pos > 0
            res += @tree[pos]
            pos -= pos & -pos
        end
        res
    end
end

def compute_depth(x)
    depth = 0
    while x != 1
        x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
        depth += 1
    end
    depth

def solve(nums, queries)
    n = nums.length
    depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }
    ft = Array.new(7) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

class FenwickTree
    def initialize(size)
        @tree = Array.new(size + 1, 0)
    end

    def update(pos, delta)
        while pos <= @tree.size
            @tree[pos] += delta
            pos += pos & -pos
        end
    end

    def query(pos)
        res = 0
        while pos > 0
            res += @tree[pos]
            pos -= pos & -pos
        end
        res
    end
end

def compute_depth(x)
    depth = 0
    while x != 1
        x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
        depth += 1
    end
    depth
end

def solve(nums, queries)
    n = nums.length
    depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }
    ft = Array.new(7) { FenwickTree.new(n) }
    depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
        ft[d].update(i + 1, 1)
    end

            r = q[2]
            k = q[3]

            ft[new_d].update(idx + 1, 1)
            depths[idx] = new_d
            nums[idx] = val
        end
    end
    answer
end
